fault_hypothesis: The overlap check on line 65 uses strict inequalities (`b <= s` and `a >= e`), which treats touching/adjacent intervals as non-conflicting. The tests expect that touching intervals (where b==s or a==e) should be considered conflicts, so the condition should use strict less-than (`b < s` or `a > e`) to detect non-overlap.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When the new window touches (but does not strictly overlap) an existing slot, the conflict check should still return True — touching should be a conflict.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) or (b__1 == s or a__1 == e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Verify that touching cases (b==s or a==e) are distinguished from the strict overlap check used in the any() expression.
result__1 should only be defined when no conflict was detected; if touching is incorrectly allowed through, result will have one extra element when it should not.

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After passing the conflict check (else branch), every existing slot must be strictly non-adjacent and non-overlapping with the new window.
